label: xLSTM & linear-recurrent hybrids

node.label: xLSTM & linear-recurrent hybrids
node.definition: Modern gated recurrent architectures scale matrix or exponential memory and linear-time recurrence toward foundation-model regimes.
node.why_it_matters: The role of xLSTM & linear-recurrent hybrids is to connect LSTMs & GRUs, Gating & multiplicative control to downstream capabilities including Transformer–SSM & recurrent hybrid models.
examples: substrate for Transformer–SSM & recurrent hybrid models
limitations: Its inductive bias, data demand, optimization behavior, and compute profile can make it unsuitable outside the regimes where it was validated. Its direct dependencies include LSTMs & GRUs, Gating & multiplicative control.

dependencies.rationale: xLSTM & linear-recurrent hybrids extends or specializes the architecture family LSTMs & GRUs.
xLSTM & linear-recurrent hybrids is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Gating & multiplicative control.
xLSTM & linear-recurrent hybrids is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Sparse, local, linear & recurrent attention.
xLSTM & linear-recurrent hybrids is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Differentiable memory read/write.
